differentials.name: Myocarditis / pericarditis with pleuritis
differentials.raw: Myocarditis / pericarditis with pleuritis (a)  viral infection (b)  tuberculosis (c)  rheumatic fever (poststreptococcal infection)

differentials.name: Collagen-vascular disease (a)  SLE (pleural + pericardial effusion) (b)  rheumatoid arthritis
differentials.raw: Collagen-vascular disease (a)  SLE (pleural + pericardial effusion) (b)  rheumatoid arthritis

context: √  cardiomegaly √  prominence of upper lobe vessels + constriction of lower lobe vessels √  prominent hilar vessels √  interstitial edema (fine reticular pattern, Kerley lines, perihilar haze, peribronchial thickening) √  alveolar edema (perihilar confluent ill-defined densities, air bronchogram) √  "phantom tumor" = fluid localized to interlobar pleural fissure (in 78% in right horizontal fissure)
